title: pilosella caespitosa subsp. uechtritzii
intro: Pilosella caespitosa subsp. uechtritzii, also known as Uechtritz's Mouse-Ear Hawkweed, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, skin conditions, and wounds. Pilosella caespitosa subsp. uechtritzii has been traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to soothe various health issues. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and confirm its effectiveness as a treatment for various health conditions.
